what_it_does: This file is an npm lockfile (lockfileVersion 3) for the instrument-surface package; it records the exact resolved versions, download URLs, integrity hashes, dependency relationships, engine requirements, and platform-specific optional packages needed to reproduce the dependency tree declared in package.json.
observations: Root package is named instrument-surface at version 0.0.1.
Direct runtime dependencies are framer-motion ^12.4.0, react ^18.3.1, and react-dom ^18.3.1.
Direct devDependencies include @types/react, @types/react-dom, @vitejs/plugin-react, typescript, and vite.
Resolved versions include framer-motion 12.40.0, react 18.3.1, react-dom 18.3.1, vite 6.4.3, typescript 5.9.3, and @vitejs/plugin-react 4.7.0.
The transitive graph is dominated by dev tooling: Babel 7.29.7, esbuild 0.25.12, rollup 4.62.0, browserslist, caniuse-lite, postcss, etc.
Many optional platform-specific native binary packages are present for esbuild and rollup across a wide set of OS/CPU combinations.
Each package entry includes resolved URL, integrity hash, license, and usually engines and/or funding metadata.
The root package entry has no scripts, license, description, repository, engines, or author fields.
standouts: Vite 6.4.3 with esbuild 0.25.12 and rollup 4.62.0 form the build core.
Framer Motion 12.40.0 is the only non-dev runtime dependency besides React.
Optional esbuild/rollup platform packages cover many OS/CPU targets, including openharmony and sunos.
Babel JSX self/source transform plugins are included via @vitejs/plugin-react, suggesting HMR/debug source mapping support.
conventions: Follows npm lockfileVersion 3 schema with packages keyed under node_modules/.
Root package uses caret semver ranges while transitive entries are pinned to exact versions.
Uses integrity hashes and resolved registry.npmjs.org URLs for reproducibility.
Marks build/test tooling with dev: true and platform binaries with optional: true plus os/cpu constraints.
Records peerDependencies and peerDependenciesMeta, including optional React peer ranges for framer-motion.
concerns: Resolved versions are often well above the requested caret ranges (e.g., typescript 5.9.3 vs ^5.6.3, vite 6.4.3 vs ^6.0.7, framer-motion 12.40.0 vs ^12.4.0), which is normal but may surprise if strict upper bounds were expected.
Engine requirements differ across packages, from node >=6.9.0 for Babel to node ^18 || ^20 || >=22 for Vite, with no root engines field to constrain the runtime.
No root license, description, repository, or author metadata is recorded.
A large optional native binary surface increases install size and supply-chain exposure.
No overrides, resolutions, or bundledDependencies are present.
notes: Npm lockfile pinning an exact dependency graph for a React 18 + Vite 6 + Framer Motion surface app, dominated by dev tooling and platform-specific native binaries.
questions: What Node.js version is the intended runtime given the mixed engine constraints?
Is this package meant to be published, or is it a private application surface?
Why are the Babel JSX self/source transform plugins included explicitly rather than relying on the default Vite React plugin behavior?
Are all optional esbuild/rollup platform packages intended to be retained, or should the lockfile be pruned to target platforms?
Was the lockfile generated recently enough that the resolved versions reflect the desired baseline?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it is a dependency lockfile for the declared React/Vite/Framer Motion surface app.
apparent_themes: Animation and interactive UI via Framer Motion
intention_signals: To establish a reproducible, integrity-checked install for a React-based UI surface that uses Framer Motion for animation and Vite for development and bundling.
novelty: Includes optional esbuild and rollup native packages for an unusually broad set of platforms (including openharmony and sunos) and explicitly records Babel JSX self/source transform plugins used by the Vite React plugin.
contribution: Provides an exact, integrity-verified dependency graph that enables reproducible installs and CI builds, while capturing the transitive dev tooling and native binary requirements of the surface app.
summary_for_embedding: Npm lockfileVersion 3 for the instrument-surface React/Vite app, pinning exact versions of React 18, Framer Motion 12, Vite 6, TypeScript 5.9, and their transitive dev dependencies including platform-specific esbuild/rollup binaries.
intention_for_embedding: To record a reproducible, integrity-checked dependency resolution for a React 18 + Vite 6 + Framer Motion frontend surface build.
